label: 1719 CE
era_summary: Great Northern War ending (Charles XII killed late 1718). War of the Quadruple Alliance ongoing (Spain vs Britain/France/Austria/Netherlands). Ottoman Tulip Period of peace + reform. Kangxi Emperor's final years in Qing China. Mughal succession chaos under Sayyid Brothers. Tokugawa Japan stable + closed.

rome_italy.summary: Fragmented: Papal States, Venice (post-Karlowitz peace), Savoy, Spanish then Austrian Naples (Treaty of London 1718 transferred Sicily/Sardinia). No unified leadership. Active conflicts: Sweden-Saxony, Poland, 1709-19; Sweden-Denmark, 1709-20; Britain-Sweden, 1715-19 Religion: Inquisition still active in many states; Jewish ghettos enforced.
rome_italy.ruler: Pope Clement XI; multiple secular rulers

han_china.summary: Kangxi Emperor's final years (d. 1722). Stable centralized rule, mature bureaucracy, peaceful interior. Tibet expedition (1718-20) active. Active conflicts: Afghanistan-Persia, 1717-19; China-Tibet, 1718-20 Religion: Rites Controversy ongoing; Christian missions tolerated but restricted.

gupta_india.summary: Mughal Empire under Muhammad Shah, dominated by Sayyid Brothers. Aurangzeb's death (1707) triggered ongoing succession chaos and noble revolts. Active conflicts: Kakonda-Portugal (Angola), 1716-22 Religion: Post-Aurangzeb, jizya repealed 1712-19; Hindu tolerance improving from prior decades but Marathas rising in opposition.
gupta_india.ruler: Muhammad Shah (Mughal), real power: Sayyid Brothers

persia.summary: Safavid Empire collapsing. Sultan Husayn weak; Afghan Hotaki invasion (1717-22) underway, will sack Isfahan 1722. Active conflicts: Afghanistan-Persia, 1717-19; Oman, 1719-47 Religion: Shia state hostile to Sunni Afghans + Christians + Zoroastrians.

egypt_nile.summary: Ottoman province, Mamluk beys ascendant in practice. Stable but with periodic Mamluk power struggles. Active conflicts: Algiers-United Provinces, 1715-26; Oman, 1719-47 Religion: Ottoman millet system: Copts + Jews protected as dhimmis with restrictions.

constantinople_balkans.summary: Ottoman Tulip Period under Ahmed III + Grand Vizier Damat Ibrahim. Peace, cultural flowering, Westernizing reforms. Post-Passarowitz (1718). Active conflicts: Denmark, Saxony, Poland, Russia, Prussia, Hanover, Britain-Sweden, Turkey, 1700-21; Oman, 1719-47 Religion: Millet system: Greek Orthodox, Armenians, Jews tolerated. Best European environment for Jews in this era.

conflicts: Denmark, Saxony, Poland, Russia, Prussia, Hanover, Britain-Sweden, Turkey, 1700-21

andalusia_iberia.summary: Spain under Philip V, in War of Quadruple Alliance vs Britain/France/Austria/Netherlands. Recently defeated at Cape Passaro (1718). Cardinal Alberoni's adventurism collapsing. Active conflicts: Kakonda-Portugal (Angola), 1716-22; Spain-Austria, Britain, France, United Provinces, 1717-20 Religion: Spanish Inquisition active; forced conversions, Moriscos already expelled.

francia.summary: France under Louis XV (age 9), regency of Philippe II d'Orléans. Stable but financially strained (John Law's bubble brewing). Allied with Britain in Quadruple Alliance. Active conflicts: Denmark, Saxony, Poland, Russia, Prussia, Hanover, Britain-Sweden, Turkey, 1700-21; Sweden-Saxony, Poland, 1709-19; Sweden-Denmark, 1709-20 Religion: Revocation of Edict of Nantes (1685) still in force; Huguenots persecuted/exiled. Catholic mandatory.
francia.ruler: Louis XV (regency of Philippe II d'Orléans)

conflicts: Denmark, Saxony, Poland, Russia, Prussia, Hanover, Britain-Sweden, Turkey, 1700-21

england.summary: George I, post-Jacobite-1715 stable. Whig supremacy, parliamentary government, Walpole's rise. Allied with France in Quadruple Alliance. Active conflicts: Denmark, Saxony, Poland, Russia, Prussia, Hanover, Britain-Sweden, Turkey, 1700-21; Britain-Sweden, 1715-19; Spain-Austria, Britain, France, United Provinces, 1717-20 Religion: Toleration Act 1689 — Protestant Dissenters tolerated; Catholics excluded from public life. No state persecution of Jews.

conflicts: Denmark, Saxony, Poland, Russia, Prussia, Hanover, Britain-Sweden, Turkey, 1700-21

scandinavia.summary: Sweden: Charles XII killed Dec 1718, succession to Ulrika Eleonora + parliamentary 'Age of Liberty' beginning. Devastated by Great Northern War. Denmark stable under Frederick IV. Active conflicts: Denmark, Saxony, Poland, Russia, Prussia, Hanover, Britain-Sweden, Turkey, 1700-21; Sweden-Saxony, Poland, 1709-19; Sweden-Denmark, 1709-20 Religion: Lutheran state churches; Catholics + Jews excluded or restricted.

conflicts: Denmark, Saxony, Poland, Russia, Prussia, Hanover, Britain-Sweden, Turkey, 1700-21

rus.summary: Peter the Great near end of Great Northern War (Nystad 1721 imminent). Massive centralizing reforms, new capital St Petersburg. Heavy taxation + conscription. Active conflicts: Denmark, Saxony, Poland, Russia, Prussia, Hanover, Britain-Sweden, Turkey, 1700-21; Sweden-Saxony, Poland, 1709-19; Prussia-Sweden, 1715-20 Religion: Orthodox state, Old Believers persecuted (double tax 1716). Western tolerance only for foreign experts.

conflicts: Denmark, Saxony, Poland, Russia, Prussia, Hanover, Britain-Sweden, Turkey, 1700-21

central_asia.summary: Khanates of Khiva, Bukhara, Kokand — small unstable states. Junghar Khanate strong further east, in conflict with Qing. Active conflicts: China-Tibet, 1718-20 Religion: Predominantly Sunni Muslim. Sufi orders influential. Minorities restricted.

japan.summary: Tokugawa shogunate under Yoshimune (Kyōhō Reforms underway since 1716). Famously peaceful Edo period — no wars, urban Edo > 1M. Active conflicts: Afghanistan-Persia, 1717-19 Religion: Sakoku in full force; Christianity banned, brutal persecution. Buddhism + Shinto state-aligned.

mesoamerica.summary: Spanish Viceroyalty of New Spain. Stable colonial admin, silver mining wealth. Indigenous societies devastated by prior epidemics + encomienda decline. Religion: Catholic compulsory; Inquisition prosecutes heresy + syncretism.

andes.summary: Spanish Viceroyalty of Peru. Potosí silver still flowing, mita labor system brutal. Stable colonial rule. Religion: Catholic compulsory; Andean traditional religion suppressed but syncretism widespread.
andes.ruler: Viceroy Diego Morcillo Rubio de Auñón

sahel_west_africa.summary: Decline of Mali; rise of Bambara states (Segou under Mamari Coulibaly from 1712). Asante Empire consolidating in Gold Coast. Atlantic slave trade peaking — coastal raids destabilizing interior. Active conflicts: Algiers-United Provinces, 1715-26; Kakonda-Portugal (Angola), 1716-22; Pirates-England (Gambia), 1719 Religion: Islam dominant in Sahel, traditional African religions widespread further south. Coexistence outside slave-trade zones.
sahel_west_africa.ruler: Various — Asantehene Osei Tutu, Bambara king Mamari Coulibaly

ethiopia.summary: Gondarine period under Dawit III (deposed/poisoned 1721). Court intrigues, regional warlords growing. Decline of imperial authority. Active conflicts: Pirates-England (Gambia), 1719 Religion: Ethiopian Orthodox state church; Muslims + traditional religions restricted but tolerated regionally.

north_america_east.summary: British colonies: Massachusetts Bay, Virginia, Carolinas. Stable but small populations. Native nations (Iroquois, Cherokee) still autonomous. King George's War a generation away. Religion: Varies by colony: Puritans intolerant in MA, Quaker tolerance in PA, Anglican establishment in VA. Native religions intact.
